meta_title: For Students: portfolio building to Solve no portfolio (Beginner Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to portfolio building and overcome no portfolio. A Beginner-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Portfolio Building to Solve No Portfolio
content.seo_description: Learn how to overcome the 'no portfolio' trap. A beginner-level checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code to build professional AI projects for your career.
content.answer_block: To overcome the 'no portfolio' hurdle, you must transition from passive learning to active shipping. By leveraging Antigravity for rapid scaffolding and Claude Code for iterative development, you can deploy three high-impact AI projects in a weekend, transforming your GitHub from a ghost town into a professional engineering showcase.
content.intro:
The blank screen of a GitHub profile is the silent killer of entry-level engineering careers. You've grinded the tutorials and memorized the syntax, yet the industry demands proof of work you don't have. The gap between 'knowing' and 'building' is where most students get stuck, waiting for permission to start.

But the rules have changed. With modern AI-assisted engineering stacks, you are no longer limited by your typing speed or debugging experience. This guide strips away the academic fluff, providing a high-octane roadmap to ship production-ready applications that prove your competence to future employers.

steps.title: Architecting the MVP Scope
steps.description: Define a project that solves a real pain point, not a generic clone. Focus on a narrow, high-value feature set.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a Senior Architect. Draft a project scope for an AI-powered document summarization tool that uses vector databases. Define the tech stack: Next.js, Supabase, and OpenAI API.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Initialize a new Next.js project with TypeScript and Tailwind CSS configured for a clean dashboard UI.'

steps.title: Scaffolding the Intelligence Layer
steps.description: Integrate your AI logic early. Don't build the UI until the core engine—the AI—is functional.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a robust integration service for OpenAI's API that handles streaming responses and error retries for a document summarization task.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add 'Create a service layer for OpenAI API interactions using the streaming pattern.'

steps.title: Database and State Management
steps.description: Persist your data. A portfolio project without a database is just a script; with one, it is a SaaS product.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design a Supabase schema for storing user-uploaded documents, their metadata, and the resulting AI-generated summaries.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate the SQL migration scripts for the document and summary tables in Supabase.'

steps.title: Iterative UI Construction
steps.description: Wrap your logic in a clean, responsive interface. Focus on UX flow over complex styling.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a React component for a file upload drag-and-drop zone with a progress bar and a display area for the generated summary.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add 'Implement the main dashboard UI components using Shadcn UI for a professional, enterprise-grade look.'

steps.title: Deployment and Documentation
steps.description: A live link is worth a thousand lines of code. Deploy your work and write a professional README.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Draft a professional GitHub README for this project, including a 'How it Works' section, tech stack overview, and setup instructions.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Configure the Vercel deployment pipeline and create a professional README.md file highlighting the technical challenges solved.'

pro_tips: Always commit your work with descriptive messages; recruiters look at your commit history to verify consistency.
Include a 'Technical Challenges' section in your README to demonstrate how you debugged complex issues.
Add a Loom video link to your repository; showing the app in action is 10x more effective than a code link.
Focus on one 'hero' feature that demonstrates complex logic, like rate limiting or custom AI prompt engineering.
Use environment variables for all keys; never push secrets to your repository, as it signals poor security practices.
content.next_steps: Once deployed, share your project on LinkedIn with a brief post explaining the 'Why' behind your feature choices, tag the technologies you used, and pin the repository to your GitHub profile.
content.conversion_block: You have the tools, the blueprint, and the roadmap. The only thing missing is your first commit. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
